arguments.task: Research Paygent Connect using official first-party pages only. Open live canonical pages on the official NMI domain that document: 1) Paygent Connect or NMI gateway API/authentication, including every documented auth method; 2) developer/API docs that show whether the API is REST, GraphQL, both, or none; 3) pricing or contact-sales pages that indicate whether access is self-serve or gated. Read the page text, not snippets. Return a concise structured summary with the exact official URLs visited and what each page supports. If Paygent Connect appears to be an NMI-powered white-label or reseller brand and official Paygent pages are too thin or broken, use the canonical NMI developer and pricing/contact pages as evidence for the underlying service, but explicitly note that mapping.

input.thought: Start browser research on official first-party docs and pricing pages for Paygent Connect/NMI.

raw_result.description: NMI-powered payment gateway/reseller offering card payment processing and gateway integrations, but the exact first-party Paygent Connect product identity is not verifiable from the gathered evidence.
raw_result.auth_notes: From NMI’s official authentication docs: API requests use Public and Private API Keys, sent in the Authorization header; for Classic APIs, keys may also be sent in the request body. No first-party evidence was gathered for OAuth2, Basic, or token auth for Paygent Connect specifically.
raw_result.gating_reason: The gathered evidence establishes official NMI developer documentation, but it does not verify a self-serve Paygent Connect signup or pricing flow. The direct paygent.com domain observed was a parked domain, so access for the assigned app could not be confirmed as self-serve from first-party evidence.

api_surface.breadth: Official NMI docs show a broad payment-gateway API with multiple REST-style resources and methods (including GET, POST, PATCH, and DELETE/DEL), but the exact subset exposed under the Paygent Connect branding is unverified.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No first-party evidence of an MCP server was found in the gathered official pages.

raw_result.blocker: Toolkit build appears possible against underlying NMI APIs, but the exact official Paygent Connect first-party app identity, access path, and branded API scope were not verified from gathered evidence.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No rate-limit information was captured from the gathered official pages.
raw_result.notable_signals: Browser evidence showed docs.nmi.com as a live official developer site and confirmed API-key-based authentication. Searches on official NMI properties did not surface 'Paygent Connect', and the paygent.com domain resolved to a parked-for-sale page rather than a usable first-party product site.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Structured fields remain uncertain because the gathered evidence does not verify the assigned app’s exact first-party identity or branded access model: 'Paygent Connect' was not found on official NMI pages, and the hinted paygent.com domain was parked. This leaves access_tier and the precise scope/ownership mapping for the API only partially supported.
